subject: Gaming
title: Streaming: Playing for an Audience
description: A micro-lesson that makes Streaming: Playing for an Audience usable.

hook.text: Millions of people watch other people play games. Streaming turned gaming into a spectator sport.

buildup.text: Twitch launched in 2011. By 2024, viewers watched over 20 billion hours of gaming content per year.

discovery.text: Top streamers earn millions through subscriptions, donations, and sponsorships. Playing games became a career.

twist.text: Viewers often prefer watching over playing. Personality and commentary matter more than skill.

climax.text: Streaming democratized gaming fame. Anyone with a webcam and talent can build a global audience.

punchline.text: Watch someone play instead of playing. Personality beats skill now.

quiz.question: What matters most for streaming success?
options: Personality and commentary matter more than gaming skill
Being the best player in the world
